title: Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee - May 07, 2026
meeting_location: Ben Franklin Place, The Chamber, Main Floor, 101 Centrepointe Drive, and by electronic participation

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public

attachments.attachment_title: Public Notices English - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf
Public Notices French - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf

agenda_items.description:
Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and remote participation details.
Accessible formats and communication supports are available, upon request.
Except where otherwise indicated, reports requiring Council consideration will be presented to Council on Wednesday, May 13, 2026 in Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee Report 35.
The deadline to register by phone to speak, or submit written comments or visual presentations is 4 pm on Wednesday, May 6th, 2026, and the deadline to register by email to speak is 9:00 am on Thursday, May 7th, 2026.
agenda_items.minutes:
These “Summary Minutes” indicate the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the full Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in the Summary Minutes are draft until the full Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Committee. The draft of the full Minutes (for confirmation) will be published with the agenda for the next regular Committee meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.
The Chair read the following statement at the outset of the meeting pursuant to the Planning Act:
This is a public meeting to consider the proposed Comprehensive Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendments listed as Item(s) _______ on today’s Agenda.
For the item just mentioned, only those who make oral submissions today or written submissions before the amendments are adopted may appeal the matter to the Ontario Land Tribunal. In addition, the applicant may appeal the matter to the Ontario Land Tribunal if Council does not adopt an amendment within 90 days of receipt of the application for a Zoning By-law Amendment and 120 days for an Official Plan Amendment.
To submit written comments on these amendments, prior to their consideration by City Council on ___________, please email or call the Committee or Council Coordinator.

agenda_items.title: Declarations of Interest
agenda_items.minutes: No Declarations of Interest were filed.

attachments.attachment_title: Report - ACS2026-SI-ED-0004 - Economic Development Rural Plan.pdf
4.1 - ACS2026-SI-ED-0004 - Document 1 – Economic Development Rural Plan.pdf
4.1 - ACS2026-SI-ED-0004 - Document 2 – Economic Development Rural Plan Summary.pdf

motions.motion_text:
Report Recommendation(s)
That the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee recommend Council:

WHEREAS rural villages across Ottawa have experienced a decline in local services, including banks, retail stores, and other essential commercial uses that support complete and vibrant communities; and
WHEREAS rural residents, landowners, and businesses have identified a need for planning policies, zoning standards, and approval processes that better reflect rural realities, including the distinct needs of farms, village main streets, rural businesses, and unserviced properties; and
WHEREAS many rural properties on mainstreets and in commercial corridors consist of unserviced or legacy-sized lots that face development constraints thatmay limit the ability of landowners, entrepreneurs, and businesses to repurpose or reinvest in rural properties for viable commercial uses; and
WHEREAS advancements in private servicing, site design, and low-impact development may provide opportunities to safely and effectively use smaller or unserviced lots, while supporting rural economic revitalization; and
WHEREAS feedback received through the City’s 2024 Rural Summit identified key issues affecting rural economic development suggesting that current zoning, planning policies, and approval processes should be reviewed to identify whether they unintentionally create barriers to investment, business development, and economic diversification in rural villages, and to support rural growth that is practical, context-sensitive, and respectful of rural character.
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that staff within Planning, Development and Building Services staff report back to the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee with:
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that staff recommend additional improvements drawing from insights about barriers to rural economic growth from their experience supporting rural entrepreneurs through the development review process to date, as well as through consultations with rural stakeholders, including business owners, landowners and agricultural representatives to further identify barriers and opportunities; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that provide an update on the progress of this work to the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee by the end of Q3 2026.

WHEREAS the City of Ottawa’s rural villages, including the Village of Carp, serve as important economic, cultural, and social hubs that support local businesses, tourism, and community identity; and
WHEREAS retail and service businesses form the backbone of rural village economies and rely on accessible infrastructure, including adequate parking, to attract customers, support employment, and sustain local economic activity; and
WHEREAS consultation undertaken through the 2024 Ottawa Rural Summit and the Rural Economic Development Plan identified infrastructure, transportation, and parking capacity in village cores as key constraints to economic growth and barriers to business viability; and
WHEREAS stakeholders specifically identified the need for additional municipal parking and improved visitor infrastructure in rural villages to support tourism, local spending, and business success; and
WHEREAS the Rural Economic Development Plan emphasizes the importance of integrating rural economic considerations into City decision-making, including infrastructure investments such as transportation and parking that support and strengthen the rural economy; and
WHEREAS the Plan also identifies the importance of coordinated, place-based economic development and investments in village infrastructure to sustain and grow rural economies; and
WHEREAS the City of Ottawa Official Plan directs growth to rural villages as focal points for economic activity, service delivery, and community life, and supports investments in public realm and infrastructure that enhance the viability and attractiveness of these areas; and
WHEREAS improving access to businesses in village cores aligns with Council’s priorities to support a diversified and prosperous economy and to strengthen rural economic resilience;
THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED THAT:

sub_agendas_items.title: 1229 Dwyer Hill Road (Ashton Pines) - Municipal Responsibility Agreement (MRA) for private communal water system and private communal wastewater system
Replacement of Septic System Infrastructure at 2761 8th Line Road (Stanley Apartments) - Village of Metcalfe

motions.motion_text:
That the Rules of Procedure be suspended to consider the following motion at today’s meeting as the matter is time sensitive as the septic system infrastructure at 2761 8thLine Road is in distress and requires replacement.
WHEREAS the Stanley Apartments at 2761 8thLine Road in the Village of Metcalfe are operated by a not-for-profit corporation; and
WHEREAS the apartment complex needs a new septic system to be installed, which due to its size requires Environmental Compliance Approval (ECA) from the province; and
WHEREAS there is sign off on the application form that is required from the City to establish the municipality's general concurrence with the proposal and to ensure that the proposed works would not contravene any municipal by-laws or other requirements; and
WHEREAS the project does not require any type of planning approvals by the City, meaning that Planning, Development and Building Services staff do not have delegated authority to sign off on the ECA application to the Ministry;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that once Planning Services staff have been provided with the full application package and deemed it to be satisfactory, that Council delegate authority to the Director of Infrastructure and Water Services to sign off on the ECA application form required to be submitted to the Ministry.
